title: The whole page, from an empty file
checkLabels.t2: The file is no longer empty
checkLabels.t11: The text does not touch the border
checkLabels.t13: The margin runs one way only
why.untouched: styles.css still holds only the comment. The whole file is yours.
hint2.untouched: Start at the top: the counting switch, then body, then the column.
why.unlinked: The page cannot find styles.css. You changed the link line.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin and start over.
why.used-inline: You used style in the tag. That is lesson 01 again.
why.touched-html: You edited the HTML. Here you only write styles.
why.no-card: There is no element with the card class.
why.no-page: There is no element with the page class.
why.no-btn: There is no element with the btn class.
why.froze-height: You set a height. It comes from the text, not from you.
hint2.froze-height: The two cards hold text of different length. A hard number will cut one of them.
hint2.no-switch: The four lines with the asterisk. Written once, at the top.
why.no-ceiling: The column has no ceiling and the text spreads.
hint2.no-ceiling: A long line reads badly. Somewhere between 400 and 600 pixels is sensible.
why.not-centered: The column sits against the left edge.
hint2.not-centered: There is a ceiling, so there is leftover. auto divides it.
why.no-border: The cards have no visible border.
hint2.no-border: Three parts: width, style, colour. Without the style the other two stay silent.
why.no-padding: The text in the cards touches the border.
why.collapsing: You gave a margin both above and below.
hint2.collapsing: Between the cards the two collapse into the larger one and the number in your code no longer means the gap on screen.
hint3.collapsing: Keep the margin running one way. Then what you write is what you see.
why.btn-inline: The button does not accept sizes.
hint2.btn-inline: A link is inline. It needs inline-block.
why.btn-no-width: The button is exactly as wide as its text.
why.btn-underlined: The button is still underlined like a link.
hint2.btn-underlined: Take the underline away and you owe another signal — the border and the button shape do that job.
blocks.0.text: Fifteen lessons about the box. Now the page is bare: the HTML is there, styles.css is empty, and everything that shows up comes from you.
blocks.2.items.0: The page has its own font and does not touch the edge of the screen.
blocks.2.items.1: The content sits in a column that does not spread on a wide screen and shrinks on a narrow one.
blocks.2.items.3: The cards have a visible border and air around the text.
blocks.2.items.4: There is a gap between the cards that does not collapse.
blocks.2.items.5: The link at the bottom looks like a button: a width, a border and centred text.
blocks.4.text: Top down, general before specific. First the counting switch, which covers everything. Then body — the font and space from the edge. Then the column. Then the blocks inside. The button last. Start from the button and you will size it against a width you are about to change.
blocks.5.text: The numbers are yours. The check looks at whether the box behaves, not at whether you picked 480 or 520.
blocks.7.text: Write styles.css from scratch. Do not touch the HTML — its classes are page, card and btn. There is no single right answer; there are six things that have to behave correctly.
